NNRT.name: Narrative-to-Neutral Report Transformer
NNRT.description: Narrative-to-Neutral Report Transformer. Converts biased narrative reports into neutral, structured format. Python NLP pipeline with multiple processing passes.

modules.name: Persistence
modules.description:
Storage, export formats, API, database

Acceptance Criteria:
- Processed reports stored with full audit trail
- JSON export of structured analysis
- PDF generation of neutral report
- REST API for ext

modules.name: Pressure
modules.description:
Bias detection, pressure indicators, sentiment neutralization

Acceptance Criteria:
- Loaded language detection with confidence scores
- Emotional appeal identification
- Selective framing detection
-

modules.name: Mapping
modules.description:
Narrative → neutral mapping engine, transformation rules

Acceptance Criteria:
- At least 10 narrative-to-neutral transformation rules defined
- Rule engine applies transformations preserving factual

modules.name: Structuring
modules.description:
Report structuring, section detection, hierarchy extraction

Acceptance Criteria:
- Section hierarchy detected from document structure
- Claims, evidence, and sources identified
- Document graph built

modules.name: Intake
modules.description:
Document ingestion, format detection, preprocessing

Acceptance Criteria:
- Document ingestion works for at least PDF and plain text
- Encoding normalization handles UTF-8 and common formats
- Section

DSPD.name: DevOps Solution Product Development
DSPD.description: DevOps Solution Product Development — project management surface for the fleet built on self-hosted Plane. The PM agent bridges Plane (sprints, epics, analytics) ↔ OCMC (agent dispatch).

modules.name: Customization
modules.description:
Webhooks (HMAC verified), Plane MCP server for PM, analytics, velocity tracking, burn-down

Notes:
Webhooks with HMAC, Plane MCP server for PM, analytics.
dspd/webhooks.py built. scripts/plane-setup-w

modules.name: Sync
modules.description:
Bidirectional Plane ↔ OCMC sync. Human creates in Plane → PM evaluates → OCMC task created → agent works → completes → chain syncs back to Plane.

Sync is fleet-tagged in multi-fleet: each fleet syncs

modules.name: Integration
modules.description:
Fleet CLI (fleet plan create/list/sync/status), plane_client.py (typed async REST client), PM agent as the bridge between Plane and OCMC.

The PM is the sole writer to Plane. Plane MCP tools (M-SC04)

modules.name: Setup
modules.description:
Docker (12 services), IaC (setup.sh install), mission seed (plane-seed-mission.sh reads config/mission.yaml), project layout, nginx proxy, env templates.

setup.sh handles: secrets generation, service

issues.title: Build Plane MCP tools for PM agent (M-SC04)
Welcome to DSPD — Plane Platform Configuration

OF.name: OpenClaw Fleet

AICP.name: AI Control Platform
AICP.description:
AI Control Platform — personal AI control workspace orchestrating LocalAI and Claude Code through Think/Edit/Act modes.
PRIMARY MISSION: Progressive LocalAI independence. Route work through LocalAI for everything that doesn't require Claude's reasoning. Target: 80%+ reduction in Claude token usage.
"Its important that the main first mission for the plane/dspd is to make localAI functional and then make it more and more reliable to offload as much as possible the work from claude till one day may

modules.name: Integrations
modules.description:
Backend integrations connecting AICP to inference providers.

LocalAI: localhost:8090, OpenAI-compatible API, 9 models loaded.
Claude Code: subprocess invocation, opus for complex, sonnet for standard

modules.name: Core Platform
modules.description:
AICP core primitives: controller, Think/Edit/Act mode enforcement, backend routing.

The aicp/ package has 46 modules including controller.py, router.py, modes.py, guardrails/. Assessment needed: what

modules.name: Stage 5: Near-Independent Operation
modules.description:
The long-term target. Fleet runs primarily on LocalAI for routine operations. Claude called only for complex code, architecture, security, strategic planning.

Targets:
- 80%+ of fleet operations run

modules.name: Stage 4: Reliability and Failover
modules.description:
Build graceful degradation between LocalAI and Claude. Agents should never know which backend they are talking to — the control plane handles routing transparently.

If LocalAI fails → automatic Claud

modules.name: Stage 3: Progressive Offload
modules.description:
Move routine operations to LocalAI one by one, with quality validation for each.

Target operations:
- Idle heartbeats → LocalAI (zero Claude tokens, just HEARTBEAT_OK)
- Simple task acceptance → Loca

modules.name: Stage 2: Route Simple Operations to LocalAI
modules.description:
Build the inference router (fleet/core/inference_router.py) — the critical architecture piece that decides what needs reasoning vs what can be handled locally or as direct HTTP calls.

Key insight: ma

modules.name: Stage 1: Make LocalAI Functional
modules.description: Assess current state of AICP and LocalAI. Get local inference working on both machines. Verify OpenAI-compatible API with standard client libraries. Benchmark latency, throughput, and quality for all

issues.title: Benchmark all target operations on LocalAI
Complete LocalAI cluster verification — Machine 2 setup
